intro: Viracocha minor, also known as Cussonia viracocha, is a plant species native to the Andean region, belonging to the arum family (Araceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Viracocha minor include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made with Viracocha minor include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia, and to support overall immune function.
